title: 1215: The Magna Carta
author: Historical Fact
content: Signed by King John of England at Runnymede, the charter established the principle that everyone, including the king, is subject to the law. It was an attempt to limit the power of a centralized authority.
action: Create a project standard that binds even you.

title: 1776: The Publication of 'The Wealth of Nations'
author: Historical Fact
content: Adam Smith published his magnum opus, arguing that the 'invisible hand' of the market—driven by individual self-interest—often leads to better outcomes than central planning.
action: Allow project components to self-optimize instead of micromanaging.

title: 1930: The Smoot-Hawley Tariff Act
author: Historical Fact
content: The U.S. raised tariffs on over 20,000 imported goods, intended to protect local industry. It backfired, triggering a global trade war that deepened the Great Depression.
action: Remove harmful protectionist policies from your workflow.

title: 1815: The Battle of Waterloo
author: Historical Fact
content: Napoleon’s final defeat. His forces were exhausted, his communications were frayed, and he relied on a plan that assumed his enemies would not coordinate their movements.
action: Plan assuming hurdles will coordinate perfectly.

title: 1948: The Marshall Plan
author: Historical Fact
content: The U.S. provided over $13 billion to rebuild Western European economies after WWII. The goal was to prevent the spread of poverty, which was seen as a breeding ground for systemic instability.
action: Inject resources into unstable project areas to prevent collapse.
